title: acer hyrcanum f. intermedium
intro: Acer hyrcanum f. intermedium, also known as the 'hyrcanum' or 'caspian maple', is a variant of the Acer hyrcanum species, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Caucasus region. Its bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Acer hyrcanum f. intermedium are said to include the reduction of pain and inflammation, as well as the improvement of cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are typically made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and may also be used in the form of tinctures or ointments.
